Title: Ustilago maydis disrupts carbohydrate signaling networks to induce hypertrophy in host cells

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mesophyll hypertrophic tumor cells in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starch accumulation in maize mesophyll tumor cells during U. maydis infection.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR-Cas9 frameshift knockout of Hap1 reduced virulence, mesophyll nuclear size/endoreduplication, and starch accumulation compared with SG200. 2. RNA-seq showed Hap1-dependent induction of starch biosynthesis genes and cell-cycle genes, and suppression of oxidative stress/ethylene-associated defense responses. 3. Hap1 is translocated into host cytoplasm and nucleus, interacts with maize SnRK1α subunits, and suppresses SnRK1-dependent phosphorylation activity in vivo. 4. Hip1 and Hip2 interact with Hap1 and enhance Hap1 accumulation/stability, influencing Hap1-SnRK1 interaction.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; genetic complementation; Lugol iodine starch staining; propidium iodide nuclear size assay; RNA-seq; in planta co-immunoprecipitation; split-luciferase assay; phosphoproteomics; SnRK1 kinase activity assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ustilago maydis infection of 7-day-old maize seedlings; analysis mainly at 3-6 dpi
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for disease control by disrupting effector delivery or effector-SnRK1 interaction to prevent pathogen-induced starch sink formation and hypertrophic tumors.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq: GSE282754; interaction proteome: PXD057668; total/phosphoproteome: PXD057676
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Core virulence effector driving hypertrophic tumor formation; affects host metabolism indirectly through SnRK1 signaling rather than direct catalysis.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mesophyll hypertrophic tumor cells in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Hap1-mediated starch accumulation indirectly by stabilizing Hap1 and enabling Hap1-SnRK1 interaction.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hip1 knockout reduced virulence, but did not measurably enhance the triple-mutant phenotype beyond hap1 under the tested conditions. 2. Hip1 physically interacts with Hap1, Hip2, and maize ZmSnRK1α2. 3. Loss of Hip1 disrupted Hap1 pulldown of SnRK1α in maize interactome assays. 4. Co-expression with Hip1 increased Hap1 protein accumulation/stability.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; virulence assay; IP-MS; split-luciferase assay; co-immunoprecipitation; co-localization; protein accumulation assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ustilago maydis infection; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target to weaken Hap1 stability and pathogen metabolic reprogramming.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single knockout reduced virulence, but contribution to starch phenotype was weaker than Hap1 and not clearly additive in triple mutant.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally UMAG_00792; renamed Hip1. Not functionally redundant with Hip2 despite physical association.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mesophyll hypertrophic tumor cells in infected leaves
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports Hap1-mediated starch accumulation indirectly by stabilizing Hap1 and enabling Hap1-SnRK1 interaction.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Hip2 knockout reduced virulence and Hip2 physically associates with Hap1 and Hip1. 2. Hip2 interacts directly with maize ZmSnRK1α2. 3. Loss of Hip2 disrupted Hap1 pulldown of SnRK1α in maize interactome assays. 4. Hip2 enhances Hap1 protein accumulation/stability when co-expressed.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR-Cas9 knockout; virulence assay; IP-MS; split-luciferase assay; co-immunoprecipitation; co-localization; protein accumulation assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Ustilago maydis infection; transient co-expression in N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential auxiliary target to block effector module assembly around SnRK1 and reduce pathogen-driven starch sink formation.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Not functionally redundant with Hip1 in complementation tests.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Originally UMAG_00793; renamed Hip2. Higher HTT expression than some other candidates and contributes to virulence.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces ADP-glucose from glucose-1-phosphate and ATP, supplying activated glucosyl donor for starch synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with Hap1-dependent starch accumulation during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RNA-seq showed AGPase3 up-regulation in SG200-infected maize. 2. This induction was reduced in CR-hap1 infection, linking AGPase3 expression to Hap1-dependent starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as host metabolic gene responsive to Hap1; not directly genetically manipulated in this study.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Introduces alpha-1,6 branch points into glucan chains during starch biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with Hap1-dependent starch accumulation during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. SBE I transcript abundance increased in SG200-infected tissue. 2. This increase was attenuated in CR-hap1 infection, consistent with reduced starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Host pathway gene highlighted in Hap1-dependent transcriptional reprogramming.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions in starch biosynthesis affecting glucan branching architecture and amylopectin structure.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Higher expression is associated with Hap1-dependent starch accumulation during infection.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ae1 was among starch biosynthesis genes induced in SG200-infected maize. 2. Reduced induction in CR-hap1 linked Ae1 expression to Hap1-mediated starch accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not functionally perturbed directly; included because specifically highlighted in Results as Hap1-responsive starch biosynthesis gene.

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: mesophyll hypertrophic tumor cells in infected leaves
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK1 catalytic alpha subunit; serine/threonine protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: protein-protein interaction-mediated inhibition of kinase signaling by fungal effectors
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: carbohydrate metabolism and starch biosynthesis repression under energy/stress signaling
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: SnRK1 downstream substrates; starch biosynthesis program; defense-associated outputs
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Normally represses anabolic metabolism including starch synthesis and promotes defense/catabolic outputs; Hap1 suppresses this signaling output.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Primary host target through which Hap1 reprograms carbohydrate metabolism toward starch accumulation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Association with Hap1 leads to reduced SnRK1 signaling and increased starch accumulation in infected maize tissues.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. IP-MS identified maize SnRK1α isoforms as top Hap1-associated host targets. 2. Split-luciferase and co-IP validated direct interaction of Hap1 with ZmSnRK1α2, and Hip1/Hip2 also interacted with ZmSnRK1α2. 3. Hap1 reduced SnRK1α2-dependent phosphorylation of the rACC1 reporter in vivo. 4. Hap1-dependent phosphoproteomics indicated altered phosphorylation of SnRK1 complex components and substrates during infection.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: IP-MS; split-luciferase complementation; co-immunoprecipitation; co-localization; phosphoproteomics; SnRK1 activity assay using rACC1 reporter
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; transient assays in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Engineering or selecting SnRK1α2 alleles resistant to effector binding may reduce pathogen-induced starch sink formation and tumor susceptibility, though core energy signaling functions must be preserved.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: SnRK1 is a central energy/stress regulator, so altering its activity or effector-binding surfaces may impact growth, metabolism, and immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The Discussion explicitly proposes generating maize ZmSnRK1α2 mutants to test contribution to tumor formation.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK1 catalytic alpha subunit; serine/threonine protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy/stress signaling linked to carbohydrate metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Contributes as one of the Hap1-associated SnRK1α isoforms, though alpha2 is the strongest validated interactor in split-luciferase assays.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely part of the SnRK1 signaling module affected by Hap1 during starch reprogramming.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by Hap1 IP-MS as an associated maize SnRK1α isoform. 2. Co-IP in N. benthamiana supported interaction with Hap1, although split-luciferase signal was not detected.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; transient assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is weaker than for ZmSnRK1α2.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: SnRK1 catalytic alpha subunit; serine/threonine protein kinase
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: energy/stress signaling linked to carbohydrate metabolism
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Associated SnRK1α isoform potentially contributing to the signaling context affected by Hap1.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely part of the SnRK1 signaling module perturbed during infection, but less strongly supported than ZmSnRK1α2.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Identified by Hap1 IP-MS as an associated maize SnRK1α isoform. 2. Co-IP validated physical association with Hap1, whereas split-luciferase did not show a visible signal.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: U. maydis infection; transient assay in N. benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Evidence is weaker than for ZmSnRK1α2.
